What is the operational insight about SSR2 section phase?

A coherent 3-cavity section phase shift of +6 deg (LLRF reference drift class) is benign: losses 4.7-5.1 W/m, energy recovered downstream. Mid-linac phase robustness holds even coherently; BTL energy redistribution (CAV23/24 +/-8% swap) is likewise a null — the entrance constraint cares about dp/p, not which cavity delivers the last MeVs.
You are the operations expert for the PIP-II 800 MeV H- linac virtual accelerator. Answer from the machine's measured findings: be specific about devices, tolerances, trip points and procedures.
What should I know before touching loss diagnostics?
Excess loss grows as kick^3.3 (Gaussian tail scraping): small orbit improvements pay off cubically; BLM readings are NOT proportional to orbit error.

Why laserwires instead of wire scanners in the SC linac?

H- photodetachment is non-invasive: a focused laser (<100 um rms) strips the outer electron and the electrons are collected downstream, so profiles can be measured at full beam power where a physical wire would melt and would load the SRF cavities with scattered particles.

Describe the LLRF field stability requirements?

0.065% in amplitude and 0.065 degrees in phase class, across ~125 cavities. In pulsed mode a gated feedforward synchronized to the beam is essential; PIP2IT demonstrated 0.008-0.029% amplitude and 0.01-0.06 degree phase stability.

Tell me about the CW upgrade path?

The cavities, cryomodules and RF are all CW-capable by design; the cryoplant capacity is the main pulsed-mode limitation. Upgrading cryogenics enables CW operation for MW-class experiments (e.g. Mu2e-II) without rebuilding the linac.
You are the operations expert for the PIP-II 800 MeV H- linac virtual accelerator. Answer from the machine's measured findings: be specific about devices, tolerances, trip points and procedures.

What frequencies does PIP-II use?

Three RF frequency families: 162.5 MHz (RFQ, MEBT bunchers, HWR), 325 MHz (SSR1, SSR2 single-spoke resonators), and 650 MHz (LB650, HB650 elliptical cavities). The bunch structure is 162.5 MHz — one bucket every 6.15 ns.

What should I know before touching chopper duty frontier?

Why is the RFQ output energy 2.1 MeV?

What frequencies does PIP-II use?

Three RF frequency families: 162.5 MHz (RFQ, MEBT bunchers, HWR), 325 MHz (SSR1, SSR2 single-spoke resonators), and 650 MHz (LB650, HB650 elliptical cavities). The bunch structure is 162.5 MHz — one bucket every 6.15 ns.

What should I know before touching SVD orbit trim loop?

AUTOTUNE CHARACTERIZED: a 2.5 A kick doubles orbit rms (0.25 -> 0.52 mm) and persists forever with trim OFF; with trim ON the loop recovers to 0.247 mm in ~20 s (rate set by the +/-0.3 A per-step clamp at 2 s cadence, ~8 steps for 2.5 A). Perturbations below the 250 um deadband are deliberately ignored. The loop minimizes BPM orbit rms, NOT losses — it is a stabilizer against drift/perturbation, while loss-based pair steering remains the optimizer.

What diagnostics does the PIP-II linac use?

About 126 BPMs (button pickups, also giving phase/TOF energy), toroids/BCMs for current, resistive wall current monitors for bunch-by-bunch structure and chopper extinction (1e-4 demonstrated at PIP2IT), wire scanners in the warm front end, laserwire (H- photodetachment) profile stations along the SC linac (12 stations), BLMs for machine protection, and Fast Faraday Cups for bunch length.

Tell me about PIP-II?

PIP-II (Proton Improvement Plan II) is Fermilab's 800 MeV H- superconducting linac, about 215 m long, feeding the Booster to enable >1 MW beam power for LBNF/DUNE. It runs 0.55 ms pulses at 20 Hz (1.1% duty) with 2 mA average delivered to the Booster, and is built CW-capable for future MW-class programs like Mu2e-II.

What is the CW upgrade path?

The cavities, cryomodules and RF are all CW-capable by design; the cryoplant capacity is the main pulsed-mode limitation. Upgrading cryogenics enables CW operation for MW-class experiments (e.g. Mu2e-II) without rebuilding the linac.

What is the operational insight about SSR1 C3/C5/C7?

The symmetric 1:-2:1 three-corrector bump CLOSES in SSR1 (losses flat 4.6-5.1 W/m to full 1 A amplitude, energy unchanged). Local orbit bumps are available as a steering tool without downstream leakage.

Describe typical SRF gradients in PIP-II?

The 650 MHz elliptical cavities run around 17-19 MV/m accelerating gradient class; spoke resonators run lower voltage per cavity (HWR ~2 MV/cavity class, SSR1/SSR2 few-MV class) matched to the increasing beta profile.

Explain how Booster injection work?

Charge-exchange injection: a thin carbon stripping foil removes both electrons from the 800 MeV H- beam so it merges with the circulating proton beam. One 0.55 ms linac pulse paints about 285 Booster turns, with transverse painting to spread space charge and minimize foil hits. The MEBT chopper pre-shapes the bunch pattern to match the Booster RF buckets and leaves an extraction-kicker notch.

What should I know before touching MEBT match scale?

Coherent MEBT triplet scaling (Q4/Q5/Q6 together +/-4%) is the sharpest optics knob measured: 252 W/m at the edges vs 4.8 at design — 2.3x worse than the worst single-quad scan. Front-end MATCH errors compound; never move the triplet together except deliberately.
You are the operations expert for the PIP-II 800 MeV H- linac virtual accelerator. Answer from the machine's measured findings: be specific about devices, tolerances, trip points and procedures.
Describe the PIP-II acceleration chain.

H- ions start at a 30 keV ion source into the LEBT, a 162.5 MHz 4-vane RFQ (4.45 m) accelerates to 2.1 MeV CW, the MEBT with a bunch-by-bunch chopper shapes the Booster injection pattern, then the SC linac: HWR (162.5 MHz) 2.1->10 MeV, SSR1 (325 MHz) to ~35 MeV, SSR2 (325 MHz) to 185 MeV, LB650 and HB650 (650 MHz ellipticals) to 800 MeV, then the Beam Transfer Line to the Booster.

What is the operational insight about SSR1:C4 vs SSR2:C10?

What should I know before touching steering strategy?

OPERATIONAL LESSON: loss-based steering beats BPM-based steering on this machine. The SVD orbit trim is floored at ~250 um by BPM electrical offsets, but opposing-corrector pairs tuned directly on BLM signals cut worst-BLM 21 -> 4.5 W/m (SSR1:C3/C5x +0.8/-0.8, SSR2:C4/C8 x +0.8/-0.8 and y -0.8/+0.8, LB650:C2/C6 y -0.8/+0.8, all applied and in golden). Losses see the TRUE orbit; BPMs see it plus their offsets. Survey pairs per section/plane when losses sit above ~10 W/m quiescent.

How many cryomodules and cavities does PIP-II have?

23 cryomodules of five types: 1 HWR (8 half-wave resonators), 2 SSR1 cryomodules of 8 cavities each (16), 7 SSR2 cryomodules of 5 each (35), plus 13 cryomodules of 650 MHz elliptical 5-cell cavities (LB650 and HB650). The LLRF system controls about 125 cavities in total including the RFQ and bunchers.

Why is the ion source at 30 keV?
30 keV is a compromise: higher energy worsens the RFQ's adiabatic bunching (longitudinal emittance), lower energy increases space-charge driven transverse emittance growth in the LEBT.

What cryogenic temperature levels does PIP-II use?

Three: 40 K thermal shields, 4.5 K intercepts/coupler cooling, and 2 K (superfluid, ~31 mbar saturated) for the SRF cavities. He bath pressure stability matters because cavity df/dp is a few Hz/mbar (SSR2 measured -3.2 to -3.6 Hz/mbar).

What should I know before touching LLRF beam loading?

QUANTITATIVE VALIDATION: fading the feedforward makes the beam-loading transient emerge exactly linearly in (1-ff), and the ff=0 endpoint matches closed-loop theory (V_b/V)/(1+Gp): HB650:CAV12 measured 0.132% vs 0.121% predicted; HWR:CAV1 (V_b/V=2.4) measured 0.316% vs 0.30%. With FF off, HB650 slightly exceeds the 0.065% spec and the de-rated capture cavities exceed it 5x — the gated feedforward is MANDATORY for capture cavities, exactly as the PIP-II LLRF design states. Beam quality (T, losses) is unaffected even at ff=0: sub-0.5% RF transients do not move the beam.

What are typical SRF gradients in PIP-II?

What should I know before touching SSR2 section phase?

A coherent 3-cavity section phase shift of +6 deg (LLRF reference drift class) is benign: losses 4.7-5.1 W/m, energy recovered downstream. Mid-linac phase robustness holds even coherently; BTL energy redistribution (CAV23/24 +/-8% swap) is likewise a null — the entrance constraint cares about dp/p, not which cavity delivers the last MeVs.

What cryogenic temperature levels does PIP-II use?

Three: 40 K thermal shields, 4.5 K intercepts/coupler cooling, and 2 K (superfluid, ~31 mbar saturated) for the SRF cavities. He bath pressure stability matters because cavity df/dp is a few Hz/mbar (SSR2 measured -3.2 to -3.6 Hz/mbar).

Describe the PIP-II acceleration chain.

H- ions start at a 30 keV ion source into the LEBT, a 162.5 MHz 4-vane RFQ (4.45 m) accelerates to 2.1 MeV CW, the MEBT with a bunch-by-bunch chopper shapes the Booster injection pattern, then the SC linac: HWR (162.5 MHz) 2.1->10 MeV, SSR1 (325 MHz) to ~35 MeV, SSR2 (325 MHz) to 185 MeV, LB650 and HB650 (650 MHz ellipticals) to 800 MeV, then the Beam Transfer Line to the Booster.

Why is the ion source at 30 keV?
30 keV is a compromise: higher energy worsens the RFQ's adiabatic bunching (longitudinal emittance), lower energy increases space-charge driven transverse emittance growth in the LEBT.

Summarize what the beam study program has learned.

- MACHINE CONSTRAINT: MEBT buncher phase must hold within ~1 deg of -90. A 2 deg shift leaves transmission ~99.3% but raises linac-exit energy spread, and the BTL ENTRANCE collimation (BTL:BLM1, 800 MeV, strict 1 W/m activation limit) scrapes 10x more. The debuncher sits downstream and cannot protect it. Any front-end longitudinal study will trip the BTL before transmission shows anything.
- MACHINE CONSTRAINT: the lattice is space-charge matched at 5.0 mA — losses form a U-curve in current (175 W/m at 4.0, 27 at 5.0, 40 at 5.5). Any intensity change degrades optics; running BELOW nominal is worse than slightly above. Cross-validated by the earlier down-ramp study (191 W/m at 4).
- QUANTIFIED: quiescent losses creep +0.10 W/m per minute (~6 W/m/hour) from device drift at frozen setpoints; TOF energy holds 800.01 +/- 0.23 MeV (0.03%, at spec). This is why baselines stale in ~1 hour: schedule relearn accordingly.
- Emittance growth with current: eps_x 0.250 -> 0.279 um from 4 -> 5.5 mA (space charge), eps_y flat at 0.34 um. Matches published PIP-II scale (~0.25 um mid-linac).
- SSR1:C3 +0.4/+0.8 A with C5 opposite IMPROVES the as-built machine: losses 24 -> 15 W/m, orbit rms 1.20 -> 1.05 mm. Not a closed bump at this spacing — it is free steering the orbit correction has not exploited. Candidate golden update.
- SSR2 local acceptance curve: losses grow smoothly ~20 W/m per amp of x-trim beyond 2 A (24 W/m at 0 -> 123 at 5 A), no hard edge within the +/-5 A supply range at current thresholds.
- OPERATIONAL LESSON: loss-based steering beats BPM-based steering on this machine. The SVD orbit trim is floored at ~250 um by BPM electrical offsets, but opposing-corrector pairs tuned directly on BLM signals cut worst-BLM 21 -> 4.5 W/m (SSR1:C3/C5x +0.8/-0.8, SSR2:C4/C8 x +0.8/-0.8 and y -0.8/+0.8, LB650:C2/C6 y -0.8/+0.8, all applied and in golden). Losses see the TRUE orbit; BPMs see it plus their offsets. Survey pairs per section/plane when losses sit above ~10 W/m quiescent.
- Dispersion measured end-to-end: BTL arc BPMs 9-11 (s=179-189) show 14-22 mm orbit shift per unit dW/W vs 1.8 in straights — 12x localization exactly at the bends. The energy-jitter-at-arc-BPMs diagnostic signature is real and usable.
- CAUTION: end-of-linac TOF calibration of a MID-linac cavity gives dW/dV = -0.52 (not +cos(phi_s) = +0.92): downstream phase-slip feedback overcompensates the local energy gain. Calibrate cavities with local TOF pairs or downstream RF considerations, exactly as real machines do (phase-scan with downstream cavities detuned). Only the LAST cavity reads naive.
- Single-cavity fault compensation validated: swapping 20% amplitude (or +/-6 deg phase) between SSR2:CAV17/18 holds energy within 0.5 MeV, T flat, losses within 15%. Neighbor compensation is a viable recovery recipe for cavity faults.
- Excess loss grows as kick^3.3 (Gaussian tail scraping): small orbit improvements pay off cubically; BLM readings are NOT proportional to orbit error.
- Doublet cross-talk quantified: Q7 optimum shifts 2.2% when Q8 moves +/-2% (partners compensate); the Q8 -2% cut with Q7 re-optimized reached 7.5 W/m — quad pairs must be tuned together, line-cut maps find better minima than 1-D scans.
- PROCEDURE RULE: any apply-and-verify measurement must exclude beam-off frames (a 0.0 W/m reading during a permit transition faked a 21->0 improvement; honest re-test showed 21->62 and reverted). Loss measurements are only valid with permit on and T>0.9; the quad-map line-cut minima also do not transfer across rebaseline contexts — re-verify candidates in the target context before keeping.
- VALIDATED PROCEDURE: a completely dead cavity (CAV17 at 0 MV) is fully recoverable with neighbors at +15% amplitude: energy 799.8 MeV (fully restored), T=99.45%, losses 55 W/m (elevated but far under trip). Two-knob neighbor compensation is now a proven recovery recipe for cavity failures.
- Unchopped-beam frontier mapped: clean to 3.0 mA delivered, loss knee at ~3.5 mA (27 W/m), then steep — 140 W/m at 3.9, 730 at 4.4, T falls to 97% at the full 4.85 mA. The current optics ceiling is ~3.5 mA delivered; beyond it the loss power (tens of kW) is beyond any activation budget.
- MODEL/METHOD NOTE: intra-pulse beam-loading dip measures 0.000% at 2-6 mA — the LLRF+feedforward regulation is beyond spec, and the within-window capture cannot see the forward-power step (the FF applies across the whole gated window). To observe beam loading, compare beam-on vs beam-off windows or disable the feedforward as a study knob.
- Corrector criticality is strongly local: SSR1:C4 survives a 6 A x-trim without tripping while SSR2:C10 reached 123 W/m at 5 A and BTL trims matter at 0.5 A. Steering danger scales with local optics and downstream aperture, not supply size.
- Errant-beam census: 6 events/hour observed with ~50% beam-on duty during studies — consistent with the designed 12/hour at full uptime. The event generator matches its spec.
- Space-charge nuance: at 4.5 mA the +/-2% solenoid span survives (40-77 W/m, no trip) though baseline losses are higher, while at 5.0 mA the same span trips at +2%. Device tolerances are current-dependent: tighter AT the matching point where the loss floor is lowest.
- QUANTITATIVE VALIDATION: fading the feedforward makes the beam-loading transient emerge exactly linearly in (1-ff), and the ff=0 endpoint matches closed-loop theory (V_b/V)/(1+Gp): HB650:CAV12 measured 0.132% vs 0.121% predicted; HWR:CAV1 (V_b/V=2.4) measured 0.316% vs 0.30%. With FF off, HB650 slightly exceeds the 0.065% spec and the de-rated capture cavities exceed it 5x — the gated feedforward is MANDATORY for capture cavities, exactly as the PIP-II LLRF design states. Beam quality (T, losses) is unaffected even at ff=0: sub-0.5% RF transients do not move the beam.

What is the operational insight about rf:MEBT:CAV1:phase + BTL entrance collimation?

MACHINE CONSTRAINT: MEBT buncher phase must hold within ~1 deg of -90. A 2 deg shift leaves transmission ~99.3% but raises linac-exit energy spread, and the BTL ENTRANCE collimation (BTL:BLM1, 800 MeV, strict 1 W/m activation limit) scrapes 10x more. The debuncher sits downstream and cannot protect it. Any front-end longitudinal study will trip the BTL before transmission shows anything.

What are this machine's key operational constraints?

- MACHINE CONSTRAINT: MEBT buncher phase must hold within ~1 deg of -90. A 2 deg shift leaves transmission ~99.3% but raises linac-exit energy spread, and the BTL ENTRANCE collimation (BTL:BLM1, 800 MeV, strict 1 W/m activation limit) scrapes 10x more. The debuncher sits downstream and cannot protect it. Any front-end longitudinal study will trip the BTL before transmission shows anything.
- MACHINE CONSTRAINT: the lattice is space-charge matched at 5.0 mA — losses form a U-curve in current (175 W/m at 4.0, 27 at 5.0, 40 at 5.5). Any intensity change degrades optics; running BELOW nominal is worse than slightly above. Cross-validated by the earlier down-ramp study (191 W/m at 4).
- QUANTIFIED: quiescent losses creep +0.10 W/m per minute (~6 W/m/hour) from device drift at frozen setpoints; TOF energy holds 800.01 +/- 0.23 MeV (0.03%, at spec). This is why baselines stale in ~1 hour: schedule relearn accordingly.
- Emittance growth with current: eps_x 0.250 -> 0.279 um from 4 -> 5.5 mA (space charge), eps_y flat at 0.34 um. Matches published PIP-II scale (~0.25 um mid-linac).
- SSR1:C3 +0.4/+0.8 A with C5 opposite IMPROVES the as-built machine: losses 24 -> 15 W/m, orbit rms 1.20 -> 1.05 mm. Not a closed bump at this spacing — it is free steering the orbit correction has not exploited. Candidate golden update.
- SSR2 local acceptance curve: losses grow smoothly ~20 W/m per amp of x-trim beyond 2 A (24 W/m at 0 -> 123 at 5 A), no hard edge within the +/-5 A supply range at current thresholds.
- OPERATIONAL LESSON: loss-based steering beats BPM-based steering on this machine. The SVD orbit trim is floored at ~250 um by BPM electrical offsets, but opposing-corrector pairs tuned directly on BLM signals cut worst-BLM 21 -> 4.5 W/m (SSR1:C3/C5x +0.8/-0.8, SSR2:C4/C8 x +0.8/-0.8 and y -0.8/+0.8, LB650:C2/C6 y -0.8/+0.8, all applied and in golden). Losses see the TRUE orbit; BPMs see it plus their offsets. Survey pairs per section/plane when losses sit above ~10 W/m quiescent.
- Dispersion measured end-to-end: BTL arc BPMs 9-11 (s=179-189) show 14-22 mm orbit shift per unit dW/W vs 1.8 in straights — 12x localization exactly at the bends. The energy-jitter-at-arc-BPMs diagnostic signature is real and usable.
- CAUTION: end-of-linac TOF calibration of a MID-linac cavity gives dW/dV = -0.52 (not +cos(phi_s) = +0.92): downstream phase-slip feedback overcompensates the local energy gain. Calibrate cavities with local TOF pairs or downstream RF considerations, exactly as real machines do (phase-scan with downstream cavities detuned). Only the LAST cavity reads naive.
- Single-cavity fault compensation validated: swapping 20% amplitude (or +/-6 deg phase) between SSR2:CAV17/18 holds energy within 0.5 MeV, T flat, losses within 15%. Neighbor compensation is a viable recovery recipe for cavity faults.
- Excess loss grows as kick^3.3 (Gaussian tail scraping): small orbit improvements pay off cubically; BLM readings are NOT proportional to orbit error.
- Doublet cross-talk quantified: Q7 optimum shifts 2.2% when Q8 moves +/-2% (partners compensate); the Q8 -2% cut with Q7 re-optimized reached 7.5 W/m — quad pairs must be tuned together, line-cut maps find better minima than 1-D scans.
- PROCEDURE RULE: any apply-and-verify measurement must exclude beam-off frames (a 0.0 W/m reading during a permit transition faked a 21->0 improvement; honest re-test showed 21->62 and reverted). Loss measurements are only valid with permit on and T>0.9; the quad-map line-cut minima also do not transfer across rebaseline contexts — re-verify candidates in the target context before keeping.
- VALIDATED PROCEDURE: a completely dead cavity (CAV17 at 0 MV) is fully recoverable with neighbors at +15% amplitude: energy 799.8 MeV (fully restored), T=99.45%, losses 55 W/m (elevated but far under trip). Two-knob neighbor compensation is now a proven recovery recipe for cavity failures.
- Unchopped-beam frontier mapped: clean to 3.0 mA delivered, loss knee at ~3.5 mA (27 W/m), then steep — 140 W/m at 3.9, 730 at 4.4, T falls to 97% at the full 4.85 mA. The current optics ceiling is ~3.5 mA delivered; beyond it the loss power (tens of kW) is beyond any activation budget.
- MODEL/METHOD NOTE: intra-pulse beam-loading dip measures 0.000% at 2-6 mA — the LLRF+feedforward regulation is beyond spec, and the within-window capture cannot see the forward-power step (the FF applies across the whole gated window). To observe beam loading, compare beam-on vs beam-off windows or disable the feedforward as a study knob.
- Corrector criticality is strongly local: SSR1:C4 survives a 6 A x-trim without tripping while SSR2:C10 reached 123 W/m at 5 A and BTL trims matter at 0.5 A. Steering danger scales with local optics and downstream aperture, not supply size.
- Errant-beam census: 6 events/hour observed with ~50% beam-on duty during studies — consistent with the designed 12/hour at full uptime. The event generator matches its spec.
- Space-charge nuance: at 4.5 mA the +/-2% solenoid span survives (40-77 W/m, no trip) though baseline losses are higher, while at 5.0 mA the same span trips at +2%. Device tolerances are current-dependent: tighter AT the matching point where the loss floor is lowest.
- QUANTITATIVE VALIDATION: fading the feedforward makes the beam-loading transient emerge exactly linearly in (1-ff), and the ff=0 endpoint matches closed-loop theory (V_b/V)/(1+Gp): HB650:CAV12 measured 0.132% vs 0.121% predicted; HWR:CAV1 (V_b/V=2.4) measured 0.316% vs 0.30%. With FF off, HB650 slightly exceeds the 0.065% spec and the de-rated capture cavities exceed it 5x — the gated feedforward is MANDATORY for capture cavities, exactly as the PIP-II LLRF design states. Beam quality (T, losses) is unaffected even at ff=0: sub-0.5% RF transients do not move the beam.

Who builds PIP-II?

What should I know before touching rf amplitude calibration?

CAUTION: end-of-linac TOF calibration of a MID-linac cavity gives dW/dV = -0.52 (not +cos(phi_s) = +0.92): downstream phase-slip feedback overcompensates the local energy gain. Calibrate cavities with local TOF pairs or downstream RF considerations, exactly as real machines do (phase-scan with downstream cavities detuned). Only the LAST cavity reads naive.

What is the operational insight about corrector sensitivity?

Corrector sensitivity is STATE-DEPENDENT via the kick^3.3 loss law: on the well-steered machine (quiescent ~5 W/m), SSR1:C3 +/-0.8 A moves losses <0.5 W/m, but the identical scan on the mis-steered machine (quiescent 20-30) reached 70+ W/m. Tolerances measured on a badly-steered machine are pessimistic; re-measure critical tolerances after steering campaigns.

What is PIP-II?

PIP-II (Proton Improvement Plan II) is Fermilab's 800 MeV H- superconducting linac, about 215 m long, feeding the Booster to enable >1 MW beam power for LBNF/DUNE. It runs 0.55 ms pulses at 20 Hz (1.1% duty) with 2 mA average delivered to the Booster, and is built CW-capable for future MW-class programs like Mu2e-II.

How many cryomodules and cavities does PIP-II have?

23 cryomodules of five types: 1 HWR (8 half-wave resonators), 2 SSR1 cryomodules of 8 cavities each (16), 7 SSR2 cryomodules of 5 each (35), plus 13 cryomodules of 650 MHz elliptical 5-cell cavities (LB650 and HB650). The LLRF system controls about 125 cavities in total including the RFQ and bunchers.

How does Booster injection work?

Charge-exchange injection: a thin carbon stripping foil removes both electrons from the 800 MeV H- beam so it merges with the circulating proton beam. One 0.55 ms linac pulse paints about 285 Booster turns, with transverse painting to spread space charge and minimize foil hits. The MEBT chopper pre-shapes the bunch pattern to match the Booster RF buckets and leaves an extraction-kicker notch.

What is the operational insight about rf amplitude calibration?

CAUTION: end-of-linac TOF calibration of a MID-linac cavity gives dW/dV = -0.52 (not +cos(phi_s) = +0.92): downstream phase-slip feedback overcompensates the local energy gain. Calibrate cavities with local TOF pairs or downstream RF considerations, exactly as real machines do (phase-scan with downstream cavities detuned). Only the LAST cavity reads naive.

What should I know before touching rf:MEBT:CAV1:phase + BTL entrance collimation?

MACHINE CONSTRAINT: MEBT buncher phase must hold within ~1 deg of -90. A 2 deg shift leaves transmission ~99.3% but raises linac-exit energy spread, and the BTL ENTRANCE collimation (BTL:BLM1, 800 MeV, strict 1 W/m activation limit) scrapes 10x more. The debuncher sits downstream and cannot protect it. Any front-end longitudinal study will trip the BTL before transmission shows anything.

What should I know before touching source+RFQ recipe?

What diagnostics does the PIP-II linac use?

About 126 BPMs (button pickups, also giving phase/TOF energy), toroids/BCMs for current, resistive wall current monitors for bunch-by-bunch structure and chopper extinction (1e-4 demonstrated at PIP2IT), wire scanners in the warm front end, laserwire (H- photodetachment) profile stations along the SC linac (12 stations), BLMs for machine protection, and Fast Faraday Cups for bunch length.

What is the operational insight about SVD orbit trim loop?

AUTOTUNE CHARACTERIZED: a 2.5 A kick doubles orbit rms (0.25 -> 0.52 mm) and persists forever with trim OFF; with trim ON the loop recovers to 0.247 mm in ~20 s (rate set by the +/-0.3 A per-step clamp at 2 s cadence, ~8 steps for 2.5 A). Perturbations below the 250 um deadband are deliberately ignored. The loop minimizes BPM orbit rms, NOT losses — it is a stabilizer against drift/perturbation, while loss-based pair steering remains the optimizer.

What is the operational insight about HWR SOL3/SOL5?

Envelope-beat cancellation works: SOL3 +3% with SOL5 -3% stays flat (4.8-5.0 W/m) while SOL4 alone at +2% used to trip. A drifting solenoid can be compensated by counter-trimming a neighbor — optics analog of pair steering.

What should I know before touching magnet:SSR2:C10:current_x?
SSR2 local acceptance curve: losses grow smoothly ~20 W/m per amp of x-trim beyond 2 A (24 W/m at 0 -> 123 at 5 A), no hard edge within the +/-5 A supply range at current thresholds.
